ksa_text: Manage test data for AI systems, including test data generation, validation, sensitivity analysis of test cases, and protection of sensitive test data.

dcwf.top_element_label: Perform risk assessment whenever an AI application or AI-enabled system undergoes a major change, when emergent behaviors are detected, and/or unintended consequences are reported.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.

top_anchors.element_label: AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics.
Perform risk assessment whenever an AI application or AI-enabled system undergoes a major change, when emergent behaviors are detected, and/or unintended consequences are reported.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: task maps directly to Model Owner scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("test.{0,15} ?:ai|model|algorithm|system"). STRM composite is strong (0.4973); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in RSK job postings or cert curricula (CRISC, FRM, NIST AI RMF, CISM). Would a working Model Owner self-report this as required?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the competency is implicit in Model Owner work (domain affinity 0.88, 1 RSK signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching systematically underrepresents risk management and governance KSAs.
